LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JORDAN ASSAF AND BEN ASSAF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED: GRANTED. DEFENDANT BEN ASAAF SATISFIED HIS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER BEN ASSAF NEGLIGENTLY ENTRUSTED HIS VEHICLE TO JORDAN ASSAF. THE MOTION IS NOT AN IMPROPER ATTEMPT TO ADJUDICATE A LEGAL ISSUE SUBSUMED WITHIN A CAUSE OF ACTION. ALTHOUGH NOT PLEASED AS A SEPARATE CLAIM, NEGLIGENT ENTRUSTMENT IS TREATED AS A SEPARATE CLAIM FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION PURPOSES SINCE IT IS BASED ON SEPARATE AND DISTINCT ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT FROM THE CONDUCT SUPPORTING PLAINTIFFS' OTHER CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar May-30-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer A. Kung, Esq., from Cholakian & Associates, 650-871-9544, for defendants and moving party

No other Appearance. The Tentative Ruling is adopted.
DEFENDANTS JORDAN ASSAF AND BEN ASSAF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Granted. Defendant Ben Asaaf satisfied his burden of production and Plaintiffs failed to raise a triable issue whether Ben Assaf negligently entrusted his vehicle to Jordan Assaf. The motion is not an improper attempt to adjudicate a legal issue subsumed within a cause of action. Although not pleased as a separate claim, negligent entrustment is treated as a separate claim for summary adjudication purposes since it is based on separate and distinct alleged wrongful conduct from the conduct supporting plaintiffs' other claims. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
